title: Itchy Skin in Psoriasis? Try These 9 Herbal Essential Oils

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils are a natural remedy for itchy skin, and they work well because of their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The main constituents of Calendula officinalis essential oils are triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin. When you apply Calendula officinalis essential oils to itchy skin, the triterpene saponins work to reduce inflammation and itching, while the flavonoids help to repair damaged skin cells. The carotenoids, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that help to protect the skin from further damage. To use Calendula officinalis essential oils for itchy skin, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it into the affected area. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing soak. It's essential to do a patch test before using Calendula officinalis essential oils on a large area, as some people may be sensitive to the oil. If you take action and use Calendula officinalis essential oils as directed, you can expect to see a reduction in itching and inflammation within a few hours. The oil may also help to heal minor cuts and scrapes, and promote the growth of new skin cells. As the inflammation subsides, you may also notice a decrease in redness and swelling. However, if your itchy skin persists or worsens, you should consult a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils are a natural remedy that can help relieve itchy skin. This is because the oil contains compounds like aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically, these constituents can calm irritated skin, reduce redness, and promote healing. It's also rich in antioxidants, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying a few drops of Aloe barbadensis essential oils to the affected area after bathing or showering, while the skin is still damp. Gently massage the oil into the skin, and let it absorb fully. You can also add a few drops to your bath water or mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil for a soothing massage.  When you take action with Aloe barbadensis essential oils, you can expect to see a reduction in itching and inflammation, and possibly even some improvement in the appearance of the skin. The oil may also help to soothe sunburned skin, reduce scarring, and promote wound healing.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils, also known as nettle oil, may help with itchy skin due to its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. This oil contains constituents like stigmasterol, oleanolic acid, and uric acid, which can reduce inflammation and itching. When applied topically, the oil can soothe and calm itchy skin, providing quick relief. To use nettle oil for itchy skin, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it directly to the affected area. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing experience. After using nettle oil, you may notice a decrease in itching and inflammation, and your skin may feel cooler and more comfortable. Some people may also experience a reduction in redness and swelling. However, it's essential to perform a patch test before using nettle oil, as some individuals may be allergic to it.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils, also known as tea tree oil, can help relieve itchy skin due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and alpha-terpineol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itching. When you apply Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils to itchy skin, you can experience a reduction in redness and irritation. To use the oil effectively, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil and apply it to the affected area. Gently massage the oil into your skin for a few minutes, then leave it on to allow the properties to work. By doing this, you may see a decrease in itching and discomfort within a few hours. However, if your symptoms persist, it's best to consult with a healthcare professional for further advice.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils have natural anti-inflammatory properties, thanks to its high concentration of compounds like eucalyptol, limonene, and alpha-pinene. These compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy skin, making it a popular choice for skin irritations. When applied topically, Eucalyptus globulus essential oils can also help to soothe and calm itchy skin by creating a cooling sensation and promoting blood flow to the area.  If you're experiencing itchy skin, try applying a few drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oils to the affected area, mixed with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil. Gently massage the mixture into your skin, paying attention to the itchy area. You can also add a few drops of Eucalyptus globulus essential oils to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing bath experience. When you take action and apply Eucalyptus globulus essential oils to itchy skin, you may notice a reduction in the severity of the itch, as well as a decrease in redness and swelling. Your skin may start to feel cooler and more comfortable, allowing you to relax and focus on other things.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help soothe itchy skin due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The oil contains constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have a calming effect on the skin. When applied topically, these constituents can reduce redness and itching caused by skin irritations, minor cuts, or insect bites. To use Lavandula angustifolia essential oils for itchy skin, mix a few drops with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it into the affected area. Apply the mixture 2-3 times a day, or as needed, until the itching subsides. If you experience severe itching or if the itching persists, consult a healthcare professional for further guidance.  As the constituents in Lavandula angustifolia essential oils work to calm the skin, you may notice a reduction in itching and redness within a few hours. The skin may also appear smoother and more even-toned as the inflammation decreases. However, the effectiveness of the oil may vary depending on the severity of the skin condition and individual tolerance.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis essential oils, also known as soapwort oil, contains properties and constituents that can help soothe itchy skin. The oil has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, thanks to the presence of flavonoids, saponins, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds help to calm irritated skin, reduce redness, and prevent further inflammation. When you apply Saponaria officinalis essential oils topically, the saponins in the oil can help to clean and moisturize the skin, reducing itchiness and discomfort. To use Saponaria officinalis essential oils for itchy skin, dilute a few drops of the oil in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and apply it to the affected area. You can also add a few drops of the oil to your bath water for a relaxing soak. When using the oil, make sure to do a patch test on a small area of skin first to ensure you don't have any sensitivity or allergic reactions. When you take action and use Saponaria officinalis essential oils for itchy skin, you can expect the following outcomes. The oil can help to reduce the intensity and frequency of itchiness, allowing you to feel more comfortable and relaxed. The anti-inflammatory properties of the oil can also help to speed up the healing process, reducing the risk of infection and further irritation. By incorporating Saponaria officinalis essential oils into your skincare routine, you can find relief from itchy skin and enjoy healthier, more comfortable skin.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oils, also known as St. John's Wort essential oil, can be a natural remedy for itchy skin. This oil contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin. The main constituents of this oil include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. When applied topically, Hypericum perforatum essential oils can help to reduce redness and itching by relieving the underlying inflammation. If you're experiencing itchy skin, you can try adding a few drops of Hypericum perforatum essential oil to a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it directly to the affected area. You can also add a few drops to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing soak. When using this oil, make sure to do a patch test first to ensure you're not allergic to it. Using Hypericum perforatum essential oils for itchy skin may lead to a reduction in redness and itching, as well as improved skin tone and texture. You may also notice a decrease in the frequency and severity of itchy episodes. However, if your itchy skin persists or worsens, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional to rule out any underlying medical conditions.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera essential oils have been used for centuries to soothe itchy skin. The main reason it helps is because of its anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. Aloe vera contains a high amount of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium. These constituents help to calm the skin, reduce redness, and lock in moisture. When you apply aloe vera essential oil to itchy skin, it works to break the itch-scratch cycle. To use it effectively, mix a few drops of the oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage it onto the affected area.  This can help to reduce the itching and inflammation, and promote healing. You can also add aloe vera essential oil to your bath water or use it as a compress to get relief from itchy skin. Keep in mind that if you have severe or persistent itching, you should consult a doctor to rule out any underlying conditions. Applying aloe vera essential oil regularly may help to reduce the frequency and severity of itchy skin episodes.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal essential oils for itchy skin

intro_desc: People with psoriasis often deal with itchy skin, which can be really uncomfortable and disrupt their daily lives. The itchy skin can be due to the inflammation and skin cell buildup that occurs in psoriasis. This can lead to scratching, which can further irritate the skin and even cause bleeding. As a result, people with psoriasis may feel embarrassed, self-conscious, and frustrated with their condition. They may avoid social situations or activities that they enjoy, or even feel isolated from others due to the constant itching and discomfort. The itchy skin can also keep them up at night, making it hard to get a good rest. This can affect their mood, energy levels, and overall well-being.  Herbal essential oils have been found to be beneficial in relieving itchy skin associated with psoriasis. These oils are extracted from plants and have natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. They can help calm the skin, reduce redness and irritation, and promote a sense of relaxation. When applied topically, these oils can penetrate deep into the skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort. They can also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger or worsen psoriasis symptoms. By incorporating herbal essential oils into their skincare routine, people with psoriasis may find some relief from itchy skin and be able to manage their symptoms more effectively.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for itchy skin
